Home»Lifestyle»Celeb News»International Celebs»Tom Cruise steals the show at Paris Olympics’ closing ceremony

The Paris Olympics closed with a show-stopping moment as Tom Cruise made a dramatic abseil from the roof of the Stade de France.

The Paris Olympics drew to a dramatic close with a dystopian-themed ceremony that blended the ancient with the modern. From Tom Cruise’s spectacular entry to Billie Eilish’s live performance, the event was a showstopper.

The closing ceremony kicked off with a futuristic twist. A ‘Golden Voyager’ from outer space set the tone, descending from the sky in a glittering spectacle.

French dancer Arthur Cadre, embodying the voyager, navigated a barren landscape before uncovering elements of humanity and the Olympic Games’ rich history, according toSky Sports.

The International Olympic Committee described it as a tribute to the origins of the Games. The Games began in Ancient Greece over 2,800 years ago.

As the Golden Voyager floated through the Stade de France, the crowd watched in awe.

Artistic director Thomas Jolly’s vision came alive with acrobats and gymnasts exploring a world where the Olympics no longer exist, according toSky News.

The performance featured a floating piano, played by Alain Roche, and a moving rendition of the Hymn to Apollo.

The highlight of the evening was undeniably Tom Cruise’s entrance.

The Hollywood star abseiled from the roof of the stadium, an exhilarating moment that left spectators on the edge of their seats.

“The most difficult thing when you love is to say goodbye,” said the president of this year’s organising committee. The president then passed the Olympic flag to Los Angeles for the 2028 Games.

Cruise didn’t just make anentrance; he made a grand exit.

After collecting the flag, he hopped onto a motorcycle and sped out of the stadium.

A thrilling Mission Impossible-style montage followed, showing Cruise taking the flag to the US.

It was complete with a skydive and a dramatic dash to the Hollywood sign.

The festivities continued with a star-studded lineup.

The Red Hot Chili Peppers performed live from Venice Beach, Los Angeles, setting a high-energy tone.

Billie Eilish then took the stage, debuting her new hitBirds of a Feather. She captivated the audience with her dynamic performance.

The evening also saw Snoop Dogg bring the house down with his classicDrop It Like It’s Hot, joined later by Dr Dre.

This set perfectly capped off a ceremony filled with musical high points. The Paris 2024 Games were historic for their gender parity.

As the ceremony drew to a close, a highlight reel showcased the best and most emotional moments from the Games. These included thrilling bike crashes and heartfelt victories.

Athletes flooded the stage, dancing alongside bands and musicians.

French band Phoenix, along with electronic artist Kavinsky, Vampire Weekend’s Ezra Koenig, and Belgian singer-songwriter Angèle, delivered a memorable performance.

The ceremony ended on a high note with French singer Yseult’s powerful rendition ofMy Way.

The Paris Olympics may have concluded, but it did so with an unforgettable blend of spectacle, emotion, and star power.

The closing ceremony was a fitting tribute to the Games filled with historic moments and extraordinary performances.
